pattern: Sorting the numbers turns divisibility into a one-directional chain, so the largest mutually divisible subset is a longest chain found by dynamic programming with parent pointers that reconstruct the actual elements.
hints: If the values are sorted, a smaller number can only divide a larger one, so the subset you want is a chain where each element divides the next.
This is a longest-increasing-subsequence style problem: dp[i] is the longest divisible chain ending at the i-th sorted value.
While computing dp[i] over earlier indices whose value divides nums[i], remember the predecessor index so you can walk the parent links back from the best endpoint to rebuild the subset.

edge_inputs.description: small set with more than one valid maximal chain
a single long chain of powers of two
single element is its own subset
pairwise coprime values, no two divide so answer has size one
unsorted input with a chain hidden across positions
two disjoint chains of equal length, either is acceptable
a large branching factor where a shorter branch tempts a wrong pick

stress.description: construct a guaranteed long divisible chain mixed with distractor values
stress.note: distinct values required; a long power-chain seeds a clear maximum the references must all reconstruct identically
